When I started studying English here, I

But he never taught grammar rules and he

Instead, he used phrases to teach
grammar to students.
Let's say we found a new grammar
structure from lesson.
Maybe that was I want to speak English.
At that time, our teacher explained the
meaning of want to. Then he replaced the
verb speak with a lot of the other verbs
on the whiteboard.
After that, he asked the students to
repeat the phrases with three sentences.
Firstly, we repeated positive sentences.
I want to do, eat, drink, go, see,
practice, speak, listen, and so on.
Secondly, we repeated the negative
sentences. I don't want to do, eat,
drink, go, see, practice, speak, listen,
and so on.
Finally, we repeated question sentences
by using the same words. Do you want to
do, eat, drink, go, see, practice,
speak, listen?
After we repeated all of the sentences,
he asked a lot of questions again and
again
and we answered those questions so
loudly. I thought that it's a crazy
method.
I didn't believe the power of
repetition. As we all know, repetition
is really boring, but it's really
effective.
After 3 months of studying grammar with
phrases, we started to speak some
English automatically, grammatically,
and systematically.

grammar to students.
Let's say we found a new grammar
structure from lesson.
Maybe that was I want to speak English.
At that time, our teacher explained the
meaning of want to. Then he replaced the
verb speak with a lot of the other verbs
on the whiteboard.
After that, he asked the students to
repeat the phrases with three sentences.
Firstly, we repeated positive sentences.
I want to do, eat, drink, go, see,
practice, speak, listen, and so on.
Secondly, we repeated the negative
sentences. I don't want to do, eat,
drink, go, see, practice, speak, listen,
and so on.
Finally, we repeated question sentences
by using the same words. Do you want to
do, eat, drink, go, see, practice,
speak, listen?
After we repeated all of the sentences,
he asked a lot of questions again and
again
and we answered those questions so
loudly. I thought that it's a crazy
method.
I didn't believe the power of
repetition. As we all know, repetition
is really boring, but it's really
effective.
After 3 months of studying grammar with
phrases, we started to speak some
English automatically, grammatically,
and systematically.

mistakes.

what we feel.

us to think. But we want to speak
automatically without thinking about
grammar rules.

grammar rules and we have to stop
memorizing grammar patterns.

Maybe that was I want to speak English.
At that time, our teacher explained the
meaning of want to. Then he replaced the
verb speak with a lot of the other verbs
on the whiteboard.
After that, he asked the students to
repeat the phrases with three sentences.
Firstly, we repeated positive sentences.
I want to do, eat, drink, go, see,
practice, speak, listen, and so on.
Secondly, we repeated the negative
sentences. I don't want to do, eat,
drink, go, see, practice, speak, listen,
and so on.
Finally, we repeated question sentences
by using the same words. Do you want to
do eat, drink, go, see, practice, speak,
listen?
After we repeated all of the sentences,
he asked a lot of questions again and
again.
And we answered those questions so
loudly. I thought that it's a crazy
method.
I didn't believe the power of

is really boring, but it's really
effective.
